title: Quality Assurance Officer (Facilities Management)
description:
Key Responsibilities

1. Quality Assurance & Process Compliance

• Review and ensure all FM processes, SOPs, workflows, and checklists comply with ISO standards, client requirements, and industry best practices.

• Validate that operational activities align with approved procedures and the QMS framework.

• Assist in the preparation, implementation, and improvement of quality assurance policies and guidelines.

2. QMS Support & Continuous Improvement

• Participate in QMS reviews, gap analysis, and process enhancement initiatives.

• Coordinate change management for document revisions, version control, and procedural updates.

3. Audit & Compliance Monitoring

• Assist in planning and conducting internal audits, compliance assessments, and vendor audits.

• Track audit findings, NCRs, and corrective/preventive actions (CAPA) to ensure timely closure.

• Verify vendor service reports, statutory compliance certificates, and maintenance documentation.

4. Data Validation & Performance Monitoring

• Analyse FM performance metrics such as PPM completion, incident response times, SLA compliance, energy consumption, downtime, and operational trends.

• Validate accuracy of CMMS/CAFM entries, inspection logs, permit-to-work records, and maintenance data.

• Prepare QA dashboards and reports for management and clients.

5. Vendor & Contractor Assurance

• Ensure vendor processes, method statements, competencies, and documentation meet QA expectations before work execution.

• Conduct random checks to ensure alignment to contract specifications and safety requirements.

• Review and ensure close-out of service reports, service quality issues, and material compliance.

6. Incident & Risk Governance

• Support root cause analysis for incidents, faults, and non-conformances.

• Track incident trends and recommend preventive measures.

• Maintain accurate records for incident reports, near-miss logs, and risk registers.

7. Documentation & Reporting

• Maintain QA records, audit logs, compliance matrices, risk logs, and QMS documents.

• Prepare weekly/monthly QA reports highlighting issues, recurring gaps, audit results, and corrective actions.

• Ensure documentation readiness for client audits, certification audits, and regulatory inspections.

Requirements

Qualifications

• Diploma in Mechanical, Electrical, Facilities Management, Quality, or related engineering disciplines.

• ISO 9001 Internal Auditor or Lead Auditor certificate is an advantage.

Experience

• 1 - 3 years of experience in FM, building services, QA/QC, industrial operations, or critical infrastructure.

Skills

• Strong analytical and documentation skills.

• High attention to detail and accuracy.

• Able to identify gaps in processes and propose improvements.

• Effective communicator with technicians, engineers, vendors, and clients.

postedCompany.description:
Fonda Global Engineering Pte Ltd was established in 1994. Our immensive involvement in the building construction industry which relies heavily on manpower prompted us to sustain the skills of our workforce to be current and relevant in order to be productive. This belief has driven us to embark on the Continuing Education and Training (CET) business since 2006.

Presently, we have two Oversea Testing Centers (OTCs), one in Dhaka, Bangladesh and another in Colombo, Sri Lanka. In Singapore, we have a BCA Approved Training and Testing Center (ATTC). These training centers conduct training courses for construction skills.

Our focus in training was further enhanced in 2015 with the Accreditation as Accredited Training Provider (ATP) by the Ministry of Manpower (MOM) to conduct the Construction Safety Orientation Course (CSOC). In the same year, we have also attained the ISO 29990:2010 LSP certification enabling us to add the following training and education services to our WSH trainings:

i) Formwork Safety Course for Supervisor (FSCS)
ii) Formwork Safety Course for Workers (FSCW)
iii) Metal Scaffold Erection Course (MSEC)
iv) Supervision of Metal Scaffold Erection Course (SMSEC)
v) Managing Work-at-Heights Course (MWAH)

Our team of trained and motivated personnel are committed to a common goal – to provide excellent service to the very best of our ability. This is made possible through constant upgrading of our skills and our willingness to improve.

We are also making every effort to provide value-added services to our clients and we strive to provide innovative ideas and solution, without compromising on its practicality and functionality.

Since May 2017, we have been delivering the following courses under the WSQ framework as an SSG appointed Approved Training Organisation (ATO) to the public:

i) Apply Workplace Safety and Health in Construction Sites (previously known as CSOC)
ii) Apply Workplace Safety and Health in Metal Work (previously known as MSOC)
iii) Supervise Construction Work for WSH (previously known as BCSS)

As of May 2020, we have added the following courses to be delivered to the public under the WSQ framework: 

iv) Perform Scaffold Erection (previously known as MSEC)
v) Perform Formwork Activities (previously known as FSCW)
vi) Supervise Workplace Safety and Health for Formwork Construction (previously known as FSCS)
vii) Manage Work at Height (previously known as MWAH)
viii) Supervise Metal Scaffold Erection (previously known as SMSEC)


Through modern enterprise operation concept we are better equipped for new challenges ahead.
postedCompany.name: FONDA GLOBAL ENGINEERING PTE. LTD.
